name: Road Running
instructions: Begin with a light warm-up walk of 5 minutes to prepare your muscles and joints. Wear appropriate running shoes with good cushioning and support.

Start at a comfortable jogging pace on a flat or gently varied road surface. Keep your torso upright with a slight forward lean, and swing your arms naturally at your sides with elbows bent at roughly 90 degrees.
Land with a midfoot strike beneath your hips rather than reaching out with a heel strike, and push off through the ball of your foot to propel yourself forward.
Breathe rhythmically, inhaling through the nose and exhaling through the mouth. Aim to maintain a conversational pace where you can speak in short sentences.
Cool down with a 5-minute walk and stretch your calves, hamstrings, quadriceps, and hip flexors after completing your run.
